title: Domain Architect
raw_jd:
About the job

Description

Leidos Office of the Chief Information Officer is seeking a Domain Architect. This position is ideally based in Reston, VA; however, highly qualified candidates may be considered for remote work within the U.S.

Position Summary

The Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) reports into the Digital Modernization Sector and collaborates across organizational lines to deliver effective enabling digital capabilities to our global employee base. The OCIO team develops, deploys, and manages innovative digital capabilities leveraging industry-leading partnerships and cost-effective deployment and management models. The OCIO is seeking leaders to drive efficient delivery and management, more opportunities for innovation, and facilitate data-driven decisions across the enterprise.

As a Domain Architect, your responsibilities are aimed at aligning IT with business objectives, optimizing technology investments, and driving the development and realization of a robust and flexible IT architecture that supports the organization's long-term goals. The candidate should possess strong knowledge of the applications architecture domain, and its relationship to business architecture and data architecture domains. A basic technical understanding of IT infrastructure and information security is also necessary. Additionally, communication, stakeholder management, and project management skills are crucial for collaborating with diverse teams and driving successful outcomes.

Primary Responsibilities

    Standards and Best Practices: Support the Chief Enterprise Strategy Architect to define and promote the adoption of applications architectural standards and best practices across the organization. This includes collaboration with Data, Infrastructure and Cybersecurity domain architects to ensure consistency, interoperability, and scalability of IT systems and solutions. 
    Applications roadmap: Develop and maintain roadmaps that outline the evolution of enterprise applications. This includes collaboration with application Service Level Managers and corporate department leaders to understand business problems & opportunities, identify emerging technologies and supplier offerings, assessing their impact, and aligning them with architectural principles and business outcomes.
    Demand Qualification: Work closely with Service Level Managers to provide architectural guidance during OCIO Demand Ideation and Qualification steps. This involves reviewing proposed solution architectures and concept of operations, assessing technical feasibility, and ensuring alignment with architecture standards and business outcomes.
    Independent Reviews: On large transformational initiatives, perform an independent review of project team artifacts required for the initiating phase to ensure alignment with architecture standards and expected business outcomes.
    M&A Analysis: Advise on HCM, Payroll, CLM and ERP systems considerations during M&A due diligence and integration planning, ensuring IT value capture expectations are viable and met.
    Training and Mentoring: Provide guidance, training, and mentoring to IT teams and junior architects and engineers. This helps build organizational capabilities and ensures adherence to architectural principles and practices.
    Collaboration and Communication: Foster collaboration and communication between different IT teams, business stakeholders, and external vendors. This includes facilitating discussions, resolving conflicts, and promoting a shared understanding of architectural goals and decisions.

Basic Qualifications

    Masters Degree in a Business or Information Technology field and 15+ years of related government contracting and commercial IT experience; additional years of professional work experience may be substituted in lieu of a degree.
    Demonstrated experience in a technical leadership role on large back office (e.g., ERP, HCM) enterprise application modernization and business transformational initiatives. 
    Experience evaluating an enterprise's back office (e.g. ERP, HCM) application portfolio or business transformational proposal for technology risks and opportunities.
    Hands on experience with using an application roadmap to align technology initiatives with business strategies for HR, Finance, or Procurement functions. 
    Excellent oral and written communication skills, including briefings to senior leaders, technical and non-technical audiences, on information technology topics.
    Demonstrated ability to coordinate across senior-level teams to persuade, coordinate, and develop consensus in a collegial and efficient manner. Ability to influence internal peers and stakeholders to promote the adoption of architectural standards, frameworks, and best practices across the organization.
    Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills and demonstrated poise and ability to act calmly and effectively in high-pressure, high-stress situations.
    Excellent interpersonal and networking skills.
    Support a full-time work location in the National Capital Region with travel domestically and internationally as required.
    Demonstrated experience in modeling the Leidos values.
    Ability to obtain/maintain a DoD clearance; U.S. citizenship required.

Preferred Qualifications

    Experience with digital engineering and architecture tools.
    Experience working within an enterprise architecture organization
    An enterprise architecture related certification, e.g., TOGAF
    Masters in a Business or Technology field. 
    Internal information technology experience at an enterprise having federal government DFARS and CAS requirements.
    Experience interacting with Executive Leadership.
    Experience with Internal Audit and ensuring SOX and other regulatory framework compliance.

If you're looking for comfort, keep scrolling. At Leidos, we outthink, outbuild, and outpace the status quo — because the mission demands it. We're not hiring followers. We're recruiting the ones who disrupt, provoke, and refuse to fail. Step 10 is ancient history. We're already at step 30 — and moving faster than anyone else dares.

Original Posting

March 31, 2026

For U.S. Positions: While subject to change based on business needs, Leidos reasonably anticipates that this job requisition will remain open for at least 3 days with an anticipated close date of no earlier than 3 days after the original posting date as listed above.

Pay Range

Pay Range $154,050.00 - $278,475.00

The Leidos pay range for this job level is a general guideline only and not a guarantee of compensation or salary. Additional factors considered in extending an offer include (but are not limited to) responsibilities of the job, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity, alignment with market data, applicable bargaining agreement (if any), or other law.

About Leidos

Leidos is an industry and technology leader serving government and commercial customers with smarter, more efficient digital and mission innovations. Headquartered in Reston, Virginia, with 47,000 global employees, Leidos reported annual revenues of approximately $16.7 billion for the fiscal year ended January 3, 2025. For more information, visit www.Leidos.com.

Pay And Benefits

Pay and benefits are fundamental to any career decision. That's why we craft compensation packages that reflect the importance of the work we do for our customers. Employment benefits include competitive compensation, Health and Wellness programs, Income Protection, Paid Leave and Retirement. More details are available at www.leidos.com/careers/pay-benefits.

Securing Your Data

Beware of fake employment opportunities using Leidos’ name. Leidos will never ask you to provide payment-related information during any part of the employment application process (i.e., ask you for money), nor will Leidos ever advance money as part of the hiring process (i.e., send you a check or money order before doing any work). Further, Leidos will only communicate with you through emails that are generated by the Leidos.com automated system – never from free commercial services (e.g., Gmail, Yahoo, Hotmail) or via WhatsApp, Telegram, etc. If you received an email purporting to be from Leidos that asks for payment-related information or any other personal information (e.g., about you or your previous employer), and you are concerned about its legitimacy, please make us aware immediately by emailing us at LeidosCareersFraud@leidos.com.

If you believe you are the victim of a scam, contact your local law enforcement and report the incident to the U.S. Federal Trade Commission.

Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, race, ethnicity, age, national origin, citizenship, religion, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, pregnancy, family structure, marital status, ancestry, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law. Leidos will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories consistent with relevant laws.

#Remote

#Featuredjob

score.skills_rationale: Strong overlap in enterprise architecture, ERP systems (FMBT experience), and application portfolio management. Candidate has deep experience with enterprise modernization initiatives, financial systems integration, and stakeholder management at executive levels. Missing some enterprise architecture frameworks like TOGAF, but has equivalent hands-on experience.
score.scope_rationale: Role perfectly matches candidate's seniority level with enterprise architecture responsibilities, executive briefings, and cross-functional leadership. The M&A analysis, roadmap development, and mentoring aspects align well with candidate's background as senior architect and consultant. Government contracting experience requirement is well met.
score.pay_rationale: The stated range of $154K-$278K encompasses candidate's W2 target range of $160K-$220K, with room for negotiation toward the upper end based on experience.
score.raw_analysis: Excellent role match for senior enterprise architect position with government contractor. Candidate's VA FMBT experience, enterprise architecture background, and executive-level stakeholder management directly align with requirements. The clearance requirement and location preference for NCR work in candidate's favor. Pay range is well-aligned with targets, making this a strong W2 opportunity worth prioritizing.

tailored_resume:
SALIL MANIKTAHLA
Springfield, VA  ·  571-215-8218  ·  salil.maniktahla@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/salilmaniktahla

SUMMARY

Senior Enterprise Architect with 25+ years of experience driving large-scale ERP modernization and application portfolio transformations across government and commercial sectors. Currently leading enterprise data architecture and system integration for the Department of Veterans Affairs' Financial Management Business Transformation (FMBT) program—a multi-billion-dollar ERP modernization initiative requiring deep stakeholder coordination and executive briefings.

Proven expertise in developing application roadmaps, evaluating technology investments, and aligning IT architecture with business objectives across Finance, HR, and Procurement domains. Experienced in M&A due diligence, demand qualification, and leading cross-functional teams through complex transformational initiatives. Strong background in briefing senior leadership and driving consensus across diverse technical and business stakeholders.
